title: Treasury Releases Two New Resources to Guide AI Use in the Financial Sector
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury released two resources—an AI Lexicon and a Financial Services AI Risk Management Framework (FS AI RMF)—to guide AI adoption and risk management in the financial sector, developed through the Financial and Banking Information Infrastructure Committee and the AI Executive Oversight Group.
key_points: U.S. Treasury released AI Lexicon and Financial Services AI Risk Management Framework (FS AI RMF) on February 19, 2026
Resources developed through Financial and Banking Information Infrastructure Committee and AI Executive Oversight Group (AIEOG)
AI Lexicon establishes common definitions for AI concepts, capabilities, and risk categories across regulatory, technical, legal, and business functions
FS AI RMF adapts the NIST AI Risk Management Framework to financial services operational, regulatory, and consumer protection considerations
Developed in support of the President's AI Action Plan
Derek Theurer (performing duties of Deputy Secretary) and Paras Malik (Chief AI Officer) provided remarks on the deliverables
why_it_matters: This represents a significant step in AI governance for financial institutions, establishing standardized terminology and risk management practices that could affect how AI systems are deployed across the sector. However, this is regulatory/compliance-focused rather than a macro catalyst that would move markets.
